content_markdown: A heat engine does $300\text{ J}$ of work during one cycle. In this cycle $900\text{ J}$ of energy is wasted. What is the efficiency of the engine?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present in the image.

vibe_explanation: Efficiency is defined as the ratio of useful work output to the total energy input. The total energy input is the sum of the useful work done ($300\text{ J}$) and the energy wasted ($900\text{ J}$), which equals $1200\text{ J}$. Therefore, efficiency = $300 / 1200 = 0.25$.
